text: Hi everyone, I'm Fred, head of product here at Consul, and today I'm excited to tell you about
our AI service desk. Consul auto resolves 50 to 80% of your internal support requests,
from access requests to MFA resets to group management. For the other requests that require
human judgment, Consul routes them with full context. This is fundamentally a new way to run IT.
Let's look at an example. Isabelle messages, my laptop has been slow lately. Consul responds
instantly in the thread by asking useful clarifying questions. Have you tried restarting it?
How long has it been happening for? Consul is pulling context from your device management
software, your identity provider, and anything that's relevant to servicing the request.
Isabelle confirms that she's already restarted her laptop and the issue is ongoing with no
clear trigger. At that point, Consul recognizes there's no obvious automated fix and lets her know
that it's looping in desktop support. Now, here's what the IT team sees in Consul.
Requests that were escalated to the support team landed the Consul inbox. Consul has already
summarized the conversation, categorized the issue, set priority based on user role and impact,
and routed it to the right owner. It also pulls device details automatically so IT knows exactly
which device is affected. The admin replies, the update syncs back to Slack, and it's done.
Now, let's look under the hood to see just how easy this was to set up.
Here's where you configure your priorities. Urgent, high, medium, low, each with SLAs.
You just tell Consul in plain English what each level means. For example, all requests from
BPs or above are urgent. Anything that blocks someone's data to work is also urgent. Consul
understands that and applies it automatically. Routing works the same way. You just tell Consul
in plain English that device issues go to Tina. When a ticket comes in, Consul's AI is also reading
the request and automatically classifies it against your schema so it lands in the right category.
To recap, Consul instantly replies to every request with useful suggestions, escalates it
directly to the right person with all the context they might need, and intelligently
automates the issue the next time it comes up, all with minimal configuration.
That's Consul. The AI Service Desk IT teams have been waiting for. It's IT's time to build.
